front: Film: Bosley Crowther was fired from the NYT for hating this film (replaced by Renata Adler); Pauline Kael's ~9000-word rave review catapulted her to notoriety and led William Shawn to hire her at The New Yorker; considered the start of New Hollywood along with Easy Rider and The Graduate

Film: screenplay by Esquire writers David Newman and Robert Benton, inspired by Godard's Breathless, first offered to Truffaut; Godard declined with telegram 'I'm talking cinema, you're talking weather. Au revoir'; edited by Dede Allen; Robert Towne was script doctor

Film: opens with B&W credits over 32 sepia photographs (credits turn blood red) then extreme close-up of lips applying lipstick; Bonnie strokes Clyde's gun suggestively at first meeting; car chases popularized Earl Scruggs's 'Foggy Mountain Breakdown'; Gene Wilder's film debut as Eugene

Film: climax — Texas Rangers led by Frank Hamer ambush and kill title characters in a hail of bullets by a fake flat tire; Warren Beatty and Faye Dunaway announced the 2017 Best Picture winner (La La Land/Moonlight mix-up) in honor of the film's 50th anniversary
Film: Theadora van Runkle designed Bonnie's costume — angled beret, mid-calf skirt and blouse with colorful kerchief — inspiring a real-world fashion fad
